title: Four Against Darkness - Deep Dive Part 3 - Twisted Minions, Dungeons, and more!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KrjMNWiU52A

Daniel (@dungeondive) examines four supplemental books that, combined with the core game, form what he considers the ideal starter bundle: Concise Collection of Classes, Wayfarers and Adventurers, Twisted Minions, and Twisted Dungeons. These supplements transform the base game from solid-but-limited into something with genuine depth and replayability. The class supplements alone introduce a dizzying array of new character options—assassins, elders, fire elves, harlequins, monks, and even green trolls for those who want chaos personified in their party.

The real highlight is Wayfarers and Adventurers, which adds narrative scaffolding to a game that desperately needed it. Character traits and milestones let players create mini-quests and personality hooks for their heroes, turning disconnected dungeon crawls into something resembling an actual campaign. Twisted Minions and Twisted Dungeons solve the base game's biggest problem—monotony in encounters—by providing d100 and d88 tables respectively that inject variety into every adventure. Twisted Minions might double encounter difficulty, introduce unusual treasures, or give minions special abilities; Twisted Dungeons might label a place "Spider-Infested" or "A Magical Library," completely recontextualizing what you'll face.

Daniel's infectious enthusiasm for these modular, affordable supplements showcases Four Against Darkness' genius: pay $12 for the base game, add a few $3-5 PDFs, and you've got a customizable experience that grows with your interests. No commitment required, just endless tinkering.

----

Which novel class mechanic appeals to you most—monk challenges, gnome gadgeteering, or something wilder?
